device.intended_use: Adjunctive lesion-triage decision support for melanoma in adult outpatient dermatology

predetermined_change_control_plan.scope: Bounded retraining of the lesion-classification model on an expanded validation cohort to address the Fitzpatrick V + VI sensitivity gap identified at 2026-Q2 deployment. PCCP authorized in K231234 cleared January 2026; this modification falls within the cleared PCCP modification list.

modifications_list.description: Retrain the lesion-classification head on a dataset rebalanced for Fitzpatrick V + VI (target: 12% of training set, up from 4.1%). All other model components, the device's labeled intended use, and the indication remain unchanged.
modifications_list.boundary: Sensitivity in Fitzpatrick V + VI cohort MUST reach ≥ 0.88 (95% CI lower bound ≥ 0.82); sensitivity in Fitzpatrick I–IV cohort MUST NOT drop more than 2 pp from validation baseline; specificity in any Fitzpatrick subcohort MUST NOT drop more than 2 pp.

trigger.description: Regional Health published bias-coverage bundle showing 13 pp sensitivity gap in Fitzpatrick V + VI; vendor remediation commitment filed.
Vendor invokes the cleared PCCP modification 'mod-fitzpatrick-v-vi-rebalance' to remediate the gap. PCCP scope and modification boundary documented; validation protocol filed.

Validation results filed with FDA per PCCP reporting obligations: Fitzpatrick V + VI cohort (n=487) sensitivity 0.89 (95% CI 0.85–0.93). Fitzpatrick I–IV cohort sensitivity 0.90 (no change from baseline). All boundary conditions met. Implementation timeline + post-market monitoring plan attached.
